query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Submersion', 'The act of sinking until completely covered with water', 'The essay highlights the submersion of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Submission', 'The act of yielding to the will or authority of another', 'Critics argued that the plan might submission the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Submissive', 'Meekly obedient or passive', 'What seemed submissive at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Submittal', 'The act of submitting', 'The essay highlights the submittal of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subordinate', 'Lower in rank or position', 'The author hoped the new policy would subordinate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subpoena', 'The summoning of witnesses or evidence before a court', 'Scholars noted a striking subpoena between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subsequent', 'Following in time, coming after something else at any time', 'What seemed subsequent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subservience', 'A subordinate place or function', 'Scholars noted a striking subservience between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subservient', 'Acting in an inferior capacity', 'The reviewer described the tone as subservient, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subside', 'To sink or fall to the bottom, to settle, as lees', 'Critics argued that the plan might subside the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subsist', 'To survive on a minimum of resources', 'Critics argued that the plan might subsist the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subsistence', 'Real being, existence', 'The essay highlights the subsistence of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Substantial', 'Large in size, amount, or importance', 'The essay highlights the substantial of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Substantiate', 'To prove with evidence', '“He failed to substantiate his claim with facts.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Substantive', 'Of the essence or essential element of a thing', 'What seemed substantive at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subtend', 'To extend or stretch underneath or opposite something', 'Over time, the community learned to subtend its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subterfuge', 'An indirect or deceptive device or stratagem', 'Her subterfuge became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subterranean', 'Below ground, under the earth, underground', 'Scholars noted a striking subterranean between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subtext', 'The underlying or implicit meaning', 'Her subtext became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subtle', 'Achieved in a quiet way that does not attract attention, often clever or sophisticated', 'Her subtle became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subtrahend', 'A number or quantity to be subtracted from another', 'The author hoped the new policy would subtrahend tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subversion', 'The act of subverting or the condition of being overthrown', 'The essay highlights the subversion of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subversive', 'Intending to overturn or undermine a government or authority', 'Critics argued that the plan might subversive the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Subvert', 'Undermine or overthrow a system', 'The essay highlights the subvert of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Successive', 'Following in an uninterrupted sequence', 'The reviewer described the tone as successive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Successor', 'Person that immediately follows another in holding a title', 'The essay highlights the successor of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Succinct', 'Brief and to the point', '“His explanation was clear and succinct.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Succor', 'Help, relief, aid', 'Her succor became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Succulent', 'Juicy or lush', 'The passage presents a succulent portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Succumb', 'To yield to an overpowering force or overwhelming desire', 'Over time, the community learned to succumb its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sufferance', 'Endurance, especially patiently, of pain or adversity', 'The essay highlights the sufferance of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Suffice', 'To be enough or adequate', 'The author hoped the new policy would suffice tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Suffrage', 'The right to vote, especially in a political election', 'Critics argued that the plan might suffrage the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Suffragist', 'Advocate of granting the right to vote, especially to women', 'Over time, the community learned to suffragist its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Suffuse', 'To spread through or over something, especially as a liquid', 'Over time, the community learned to suffuse its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Suggest', 'To propose or indicate', '“The evidence suggests a connection between the two cases.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Suggestible', 'Susceptible to influence by suggestion', 'Over time, the community learned to suggestible its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sully', 'To soil, stain or tarnish', 'She spoke sully, leaving little doubt about her position.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sumptuous', 'Magnificent and splendid, suggesting great expense', 'What seemed sumptuous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Sunder', 'To separate', 'The author hoped the new policy would sunder tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Superabundance', 'More than sufficient, excessive', 'Scholars noted a striking superabundance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Superadd', 'To add on top of a previous addition', 'Critics argued that the plan might superadd the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Superannuate', 'To allow to retire from office on a pension because of age', 'Over time, the community learned to superannuate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Supercilious', 'Behaving or looking as though one thinks one is superior', 'What seemed supercilious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Superficial', 'Existing or occurring at or on the surface', 'What seemed superficial at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Superfluity', 'The quality or state of being in excess', 'Her superfluity became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Superfluous', 'Unnecessary or excessive', '“The report contained superfluous details.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Superintend', 'To oversee the work of others, to supervise', 'The author hoped the new policy would superintend tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Superlative', 'Exceptionally good, of the highest quality, superb', 'What seemed superlative at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Supernumerary', 'Extra, beyond the standard or prescribed amount', 'Scholars noted a striking supernumerary between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
